title: lepidium heterophyllum subsp. heterophyllum
intro: Lepidium heterophyllum subsp. heterophyllum, also known as Himalayan pennywort or Makoi, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant, diuretic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and aiding in the management of kidney stones and urinary tract infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as a natural remedy for various ailments.
